title: Abutilon orbiculatum
intro: Abutilon orbiculatum, also known as the flowering maple or Chinese bellflower, is a flowering plant species in the mallow family Malvaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Abutilon orbiculatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Abutilon orbiculatum is commonly prepared as a tea or used in combination with other herbs in formulas to treat conditions such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Abutilon orbiculatum is classified within the Malvaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This family is part of the order Malvales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Abutilon orbiculatum is a species of flowering plant native to Australia, and its classification is also related to its botanical name. The Malvaceae family comprises over 2,300 species of plants, including hibiscus, okra, and cotton.
